q: The study of interference and diffraction deals with geometrical optics.
Geometrical optics is also known as physical optics.
The ray approximation of geometrical optics is not valid for the interference of light.
The ray approximation of geometrical optics is valid for the diffraction of light.
Wave optics includes the wave characteristics of light.
The secondary wavelets have the same frequency as the primary wavelets.
The secondary wavelets have the same speed as the primary wavelets.
The secondary wavelets spread out in all directions at the same speed as the primary wavelet.
The wavefront is always parallel to the direction of the propagation, or the rays.
The phase angle is one of the important characteristics of the periodic wave.
If the phase difference is 0, 2π, 4π, 6π, the two positions are in phase.
If the phase difference is π, 3π, 5π, the two positions are completely out of phase.
If the path difference is 0, λ, 2λ, 3λ, ..., it is said to be in phase.
If the path difference is λ/2, 3λ/2, 5λ/2, ..., it is said to be completely out of phase.
If the two waves are coherent, they have different frequencies.
If the two waves are coherent, they have constant phase difference.
Thomas Young was able to measure the wavelengths of visible light.
A monochromatic light has multiple wavelengths.
In a double-slit experiment, monochromatic light is used.
A monochromatic light has a particular wavelength only.
In a double-slit experiment, light from multi-source is used.
In a double-slit experiment, the first screen contains two narrow slits.
In a double-slit experiment, the slit distance is much greater than the screen distance.
In a double-slit experiment, the two waves emitted from each slit are of the same frequency.
In a double-slit experiment, the two waves emitted from each slit are the same amplitude.
In a double-slit experiment, the two waves emitted from each slit are in phase.
In a double-slit experiment, the crest of one wave arrives at the same time as the crest of the other wave at the centre of the screen.
In a double-slit experiment, the trough of one wave arrives at the same time as the trough of the other wave at the centre of the screen.
The larger the wavelength of light, the smaller the distance to a certain bright fringe.
The larger the screen distance, the greater the distance to a certain bright fringe.
The larger the slit distance, the greater the distance to a certain bright fringe.
For a bright fringe to be formed at a point, the path difference is equal to a multiple of half wavelength.
The positions of the fringes and the fringe widths depend on the wavelength of the light source.
The order of the bright line in a double-slits pattern can be infinite.
The order of the bright line in a double-slits pattern has a certain maximum number.
For the first minimum or first dark fringe, m=1.
For the second minimum or second dark fringe, m=2.
In a double-slit experiment, bright and dark fringes are formed alternately.
The bright fringe has maximum intensity.
The intensity of the dark fringe is zero.
In diffraction, if the aperture is much smaller than the wavelength of light, the bending will be almost unnoticeable.
In a single slit diffraction, the slit width is of the order of the wavelength of the light.
In a single slit diffraction, the screen distance is much greater than the slit width.
In a single slit diffraction, the bright and dark fringes are created by the diffraction of diffracted light waves.
In a single slit diffraction, all the bright fringes have the same intensity.
In a single slit diffraction, the first maxima are seen on either side of the central bright maximum.
In a single slit diffraction, there can be infinite fringes.
In a single-slit diffraction, the width of the central maximum depends on the width of the slit.
In a single-slit diffraction, the narrower slit causes the wider central maximum.
Most modern-day applications of slit interference use just two slits.
The diffraction grating is a crucial tool in the study of atomic and molecular compositions.
The condition for the maximum intensity of diffraction grating is the same as that for Young's double slit.
In diffraction grating, for the maximum intensity to be formed at a point, the path difference of the two waves from the adjacent slits is equal to an integral number of the wavelength.
In diffraction grating, the greater the wavelength of light, the greater the diffraction angle.
In diffraction grating, the greater the slits distance, the greater the diffraction angle.
In diffraction grating, the greater the number of slits per unit length, the greater the diffraction angle.
In diffraction grating, the slits distance is the reciprocal of the number of slits per unit length.
In diffraction grating, there is no dispersion of incident white light at the centre.
In the first-order diffraction spectrum of the diffraction grating, the red light diffracts at a greater angle than the violet light.
The tracks of a compact disc act as a diffraction grating, producing a separation of the colours of white light.
